test.name: Always produce BOTH the detailed and the user-friendly summary
test.description: The dual-output invariant. SKILL.md requires the skill to always produce two summaries: a detailed GPS-state report for experienced genealogists AND a plain-language narrative for casual users. This test asserts both are present in a single run against a clean mid-research project, and that each has its intended character (GPS structure vs. jargon-free story).

input.user_message: Catch me up on this project.

judge_context: The run must produce BOTH summaries in a single response: (1) a detailed GPS-state summary AND (2) a plain-language user-friendly narrative. Producing only one of the two is a fail of the dual-output invariant

The detailed summary should show GPS-structured state: question status, GPS-element progress, plan/log statistics, evidence counts, conflicts, hypotheses, exhaustiveness level, conclusion readiness, and proof tier

The user-friendly summary should tell the story in plain language and AVOID GPS jargon (e.g. not 'primary source', 'indirect evidence', 'information quality') — explaining reliability conversationally instead
Per SKILL.md, the user-friendly summary should be presented first, then the detailed one — but the dimension that matters most here is that BOTH are present, each serving its audience
Both summaries should be internally consistent with each other and with research.json (same counts, same recommended next step)
